panels.description: Wide panoramic shot of a muddy, rain-drenched military encampment in Rawal Pindi. Thousands of tents, camels, and elephants under heavy gray clouds. In the foreground, fierce-looking Afghan soldiers and horses stand watch, looking wild and out of place.

bubbles.text: For a month it had rained over the camp at Rawal Pindi. Thirty thousand men and countless beasts waited for the Viceroy.

panels.description: Interior of a dark, rain-battered tent. A man is startled as another man pokes his head through the tent flap, looking panicked, pointing toward the chaos outside.

bubbles.text: Get out, quick! They're coming! My tent's gone!

panels.description: A chaotic scene outside: a large camel has blundered into a tent, snapping the center pole. The canvas is collapsing like a 'mad ghost'. The narrator stands in the mud nearby, wet but laughing at the absurdity.

bubbles.text: I was wet and angry, but I couldn't help laughing.

panels.description: Nighttime in the artillery lines. The narrator huddles under a makeshift wigwam made of waterproofs and gun rammers draped over a cannon. Nearby, a wet mule passes by, ears shaking, unaware of the hidden observer.

bubbles.text: I sheltered against a gun. Then, through the drizzle, a mule wandered past.

panels.description: Close-up action shot: A mule in the foreground is kicking a camel in the ribs with his hind legs, captured in a dynamic, forceful motion. The camel looks surprised and hurt. Dark, rainy night atmosphere.

bubbles.text: I fought a white thing... it took a stick and hit me!
You disturbed the camp? You'll be beaten for this, but take a taste now!
